description: UI Improvements and Typo Forgiveness - Display radical images, support reading tags, show user level, implement typo tolerance, mark as correct functionality, and component display on incorrect answers

userStories.title: Fix radicals displaying '?' instead of image
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see radical images for radicals without Unicode characters so that I can learn all radicals visually.
acceptanceCriteria: Radicals with `characters: null` display their image from `character_images` array
Image fetched from WaniKani CDN URL (prefer SVG with `inline_styles: true`)
Image displays at appropriate size (24x24 matching current character font size)
Radical images are cached locally for offline use
Fallback to meaning text if no image available
Regression test added that verifies radicals without characters render correctly
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
userStories.notes: Implemented RadicalImage component with image caching via React Native's Image.prefetch. Uses PNG when available, falls back to SVG with inline_styles.

userStories.title: Support <reading> tags in MnemonicText with italic rendering
userStories.description: As a user, I want reading hints in mnemonics to be displayed in italics so they stand out from regular text without being too visually heavy.
acceptanceCriteria: `<reading>text</reading>` tags render as italic text (no color, no bold)
Works in lesson screens (LessonCard mnemonics)
Works in incorrect answer feedback screens (LessonQuiz, ReviewSession)
Existing tag types (`<radical>`, `<kanji>`, `<vocabulary>`) continue to work
Unit tests added for `<reading>` tag parsing
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
userStories.notes: Added <reading> tag support to MnemonicText regex pattern, renders with fontStyle: italic (no color, no bold). MnemonicText is used in LessonCard, LessonQuiz, and ReviewSession.

userStories.title: Move 'made up of' section below kanji display in lessons
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the kanji character first before seeing its component radicals, so the visual flow matches how I learn (whole then parts).
acceptanceCriteria: 'Made up of' section appears below the pink kanji character block
Section still only appears for kanji items with component radicals
Visual styling remains unchanged (light blue background, radical pills)
Swipe gestures continue to work correctly
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Moved component radicals display from LessonBatch to LessonCard. The 'Made up of' section now renders directly below the header (character block) inside LessonCard, using the componentRadicals prop.

userStories.title: Display current level on home screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my current WaniKani level on the home screen so I can track my progress at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: User level displayed in the stats area near lessons/reviews counts
Level fetched during initial sync and cached
Display format: 'Level X' with subtle styling (not dominant)
Level updates after successful sync
Handles loading state gracefully (shows placeholder or nothing)
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added user_level column to sync_status table with migration. Level is cached when getUserLevel() is called during sync. LevelIndicator component displays 'Level X' above the dashboard stats. Returns null during loading (renders nothing).

userStories.title: Implement Damerau-Levenshtein distance algorithm
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Damerau-Levenshtein distance algorithm to enable typo forgiveness for meaning answers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create damerauLevenshtein function in src/utils/stringDistance.ts
Algorithm handles insertions, deletions, substitutions, and transpositions
Returns integer edit distance between two strings
Performance optimized with early termination when distance exceeds threshold
Comprehensive unit tests covering various typo scenarios
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
userStories.notes: Implemented damerauLevenshtein and isWithinDistance functions in src/utils/stringDistance.ts. Uses optimal string alignment variant with early termination via maxDistance option. 64 comprehensive tests covering insertions, deletions, substitutions, transpositions, WaniKani-specific scenarios, and edge cases.

userStories.title: Integrate typo forgiveness into answer validation
userStories.description: As a user, I want minor typos in my meaning answers to be accepted so that I'm not penalized for typing mistakes when I know the correct answer.
acceptanceCriteria: Answers within acceptable edit distance are marked correct
Tolerance: 1 edit for words 4-6 characters, 2 edits for words 7+ characters, exact match only for 1-3 chars
Only applies to meaning answers (not reading/hiragana answers)
Exact matches still prioritized over fuzzy matches
Blacklisted meanings still rejected even if within edit distance
Validation result includes `isFuzzyMatch` flag when typo forgiveness applied
Unit tests cover various typo scenarios
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
userStories.notes: Integrated Damerau-Levenshtein distance into validateMeaningAnswer(). Added getAllowedEditDistance() helper for word-length-based tolerance. Exact matches checked first, then fuzzy matches. Blacklist uses exact match only. Added isFuzzyMatch flag to MeaningValidationResult. 40+ new tests for typo scenarios.

userStories.title: Show yellow feedback for typo-forgiven answers
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see yellow/amber feedback when my answer was accepted via typo forgiveness so I know to be more careful.
acceptanceCriteria: When answer accepted via typo forgiveness, show yellow/amber background instead of green
Yellow feedback indicates 'accepted but be careful' to the user
Works in both LessonQuiz and ReviewSession components
Green feedback still shown for exact matches
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added COLORS.feedback.fuzzyMatch (#F59E0B amber) color. LessonQuiz and ReviewSession track isFuzzyMatch state from validateMeaningAnswer(). When isFuzzyMatch is true, shows yellow/amber header with 'Close enough!' label instead of green 'Correct!'. Tests verify fuzzy match vs exact match display.

userStories.title: Add 'Mark as Correct' button on incorrect answer screen
userStories.description: As a user, I want to mark my answer as correct when I made a typo that wasn't caught by typo forgiveness, so I don't lose SRS progress for answers I actually knew.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Mark as Correct' button appears alongside 'Continue' button on incorrect feedback
Tapping 'Mark as Correct' treats the answer as correct (updates SRS accordingly)
Button has distinct but secondary styling (not as prominent as Continue)
Works in both LessonQuiz and ReviewSession screens
No confirmation dialog needed (proceeds immediately)
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Added 'Mark as Correct' button to incorrect feedback screens in both LessonQuiz and ReviewSession. Button uses secondary styling (gray outline) alongside the Continue button. When pressed: treats answer as correct (updates SRS/progress), removes re-queued question from queue, and proceeds immediately.

userStories.title: Extract reusable component display for radicals/kanji
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable component to display radical or kanji items with their characters/images and meanings for use on incorrect answer screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ComponentDisplay component that renders radical or kanji with character and meaning
Handles radicals without Unicode characters (uses images per US-001)
Supports both blue (radical) and pink (kanji) color schemes
Unit tests verify rendering for both radical and kanji items
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
userStories.notes: Created ComponentDisplay component that renders radical/kanji items with character (or RadicalImage) and meaning/reading. Uses SUBJECT_COLORS for blue (radical) or pink (kanji) background. Includes displayText prop for showing reading instead of meaning. 9 unit tests cover all acceptance criteria.

userStories.title: Show component radicals on incorrect kanji answer screen
userStories.description: As a user, when I get a kanji answer wrong, I want to see the radicals that make up the kanji so I can better understand and remember it.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Made up of' section displays below mnemonic on incorrect kanji answers
Shows each component radical with character/image and meaning
Uses same visual style as lesson screen (blue pills)
Only appears for kanji items (not radicals or vocabulary)
Works in both LessonQuiz and ReviewSession screens
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Show component kanji on incorrect vocabulary answer screen
userStories.description: As a user, when I get a vocabulary answer wrong, I want to see the kanji that make up the word with their relevant meaning or reading.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Made up of' section displays below mnemonic on incorrect vocabulary answers
For meaning questions: shows each kanji character + its primary meaning
For reading questions: shows each kanji character + its primary reading
Uses pink kanji color scheme for consistency
Only appears for vocabulary items with component kanji
Gracefully handles vocabulary without kanji (kana-only words)
Works in both LessonQuiz and ReviewSession screens
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Block submission of invalid reading answers with shake feedback
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to prevent me from submitting invalid reading answers (empty or containing non-hiragana characters) so I can fix my input without losing progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty reading answers are not submitted
Reading answers containing non-hiragana characters (romaji, katakana, etc.) are not submitted
When submission is blocked, input field shakes briefly (animation feedback)
User can continue typing to fix their answer after shake
Shake animation is subtle but noticeable (150-300ms duration)
Works in both LessonQuiz and ReviewSession screens
Unit tests verify invalid inputs are blocked
All tests pass
All quality checks pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
